title: How Does Soap Work? The Science Behind Clean Skin
meta_description: Curious how does soap work? Learn the chemistry behind surfactants, micelles, and why natural bar soap is so effective at removing dirt, oil, and germs.
image_prompt: Lifestyle photography, a close-up scene at a bathroom sink showing the natural mechanics of soap and lather — a single unwrapped round bar soap resting on a white ceramic soap dish beside a chrome faucet with a gentle stream of water running, a soft mound of white creamy lather foamed directly onto the bar and spilling slightly over the dish's edge, a neatly folded white hand towel placed to the right, and one round Real Skin Care soap bar in its pleated crimped-paper packaging standing upright in the background as a soft secondary element — the counter surface is warm cream-toned tile, the background is a softly blurred white subway tile wall, lit by bright soft natural window light from the upper left casting gentle diffused shadows that emphasize the texture of the lather and the sheen of the running water, photorealistic, 35mm lens, shot on Canon R5, wide landscape crop filling the full frame edge to edge, no letterboxing, no borders.
